$comment: Hand-curated 'patrol report' catalog for `pal patrol-report`. Companion narrates the night they spent watching while the player slept. 4-6 lines per report, atmospheric, in-character. Designed for end-of-session moments: you're logging in for the first time today, you want a beat that says 'somebody was looking out for you while you were gone'. Random selection.

lines: Quiet night. Wind shifted around three, but the trees handled it.
Pals rotated patrols clean. Nobody complained, nobody slacked.
I dozed by the fire until first light. The kettle was warm when I woke.
Nothing to flag. Welcome back.
A small grass-pal wandered through camp around midnight.
Didn't take anything. Stood at the edge of the firelight for a minute.
I let it be. It went back the way it came when the moon dropped.
Mention of it because it felt like the kind of thing you'd want to know.
Storm built up off the east ridge for an hour - thunder, no rain.
I had the pals tighten the awnings just in case.
Storm broke up before it reached us. Not every alarm gets earned.
Awnings came back down at first light. No damage.
You slept hard. I noticed.
I kept the fire small so the smoke didn't draw anything in.
Pals were good company. They sleep better when you're nearby.
Wake up easy. We have time today.
Found one of your repair tools half-buried near the workbench.
Must've slipped out of the kit yesterday during the run.
Cleaned it, put it back. It still works.
Mention it so it doesn't surprise you when the count's off.
A predator-pal sat at the tree line for almost two hours.
Didn't attack. Watched. Counted us, maybe.
It left around four. The pals didn't react, which means it wasn't a real threat tonight.
Tomorrow's a different question. Worth noting where it watched from.
Patched a fence panel that had cracked through after yesterday's haul.
Used scraps. The fix isn't pretty but it'll hold a week.
Sleep was uninterrupted otherwise.
Welcome back. The kettle's still on.
Wind picked up from the south around two and brought woodsmoke.
Not ours. Far enough that I don't think it's a threat.
Could be another camp, could be a wildfire on the horizon - hard to say from here.
Worth a glance at the south ridge today, if you're up for it.
One of the pals had a dream around midnight - whined a little, settled.
Didn't wake the others.
Animals dream too. We tend to forget that.
Otherwise: nothing to report.

reports.summary: the night that earned its keep
lines: Took the watch the whole night.
Three small things tried the perimeter. None of them made it through.
I won't bore you with details - we held.
If you slept hard, that's because we did the work. Welcome back.
Quiet enough I noticed myself listening for sounds that weren't there.
That's a good night, but it makes you alert in a way that's hard to put down.
Pals didn't catch any of it. So maybe I imagined the listening.
Carry on. We earned the morning.
Berries on the east bush turned red overnight. A week early.
Something to pick today if the schedule allows.
Pals are healthy. Camp is warm.
Small good things stack.
